channel.title: Youty Updates
channel.description: Auto-update feed for Youty — macOS YouTube/Instagram/TikTok to-vault tool.

item.title: Youty 1.4.3
item.description:
Youty 1.4.3

Instagram and TikTok saves are now atomic. If a download or frame extraction fails partway, the half-finished save is cleaned up instead of leaving a transcript-only entry with no frames behind. The YouTube path is unchanged — it still keeps your transcript and offers a one-tap frame retry.

item.title: Youty 1.4.2
item.description:
Youty 1.4.2

Indexing is now self-healing. If the app was closed right after saving a video, that video could end up saved but unsearchable; Youty now reconciles the index on launch — any missed video is indexed automatically — and indexes fresh saves at a higher priority so they complete reliably even if you quit soon after.

item.title: Youty 1.4.1
item.description:
Youty 1.4.1

Youty now wires reliably into every local AI client. The MCP setup uses uvx's full path so GUI apps (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline) reliably find it, adds Antigravity to the auto-wired clients, and a single-instance guard protects your vault index from a second running copy.

item.title: Youty 1.4.0
item.description:
Youty 1.4.0

One-command MCP setup: run `uvx youty-mcp@latest install` to auto-wire Youty into every AI client on your Mac — Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, and more. The first-run setup screen now offers this one-click path, with per-client manual config as a fallback.

item.title: Youty 1.3.2
item.description:
Youty 1.3.2

Fixes YouTube transcript fetching. Public videos that have captions could fail with a 'couldn't read this page' error — most often on the first fetch right after launch. Youty now uses the correct browser identity and automatically retries a transient first-load miss, so it reads the page reliably.

item.title: Youty 1.3.1
item.description:
Youty 1.3.1

Patch: fixes a schema-migration bug in 1.3.0 that could fail on launch for an index containing orphaned entries (and would have blocked indexing for those users). Also hardens the automatic on-screen-text backfill so a partial or interrupted run recovers cleanly on the next launch instead of skipping work.

item.title: Youty 1.3.0
item.description:
Youty 1.3.0

Search what your videos show, not just what they say. On-device OCR (Apple Vision) now indexes on-screen text — slides, code, terminal output, labels — so you can find moments that were never spoken aloud, all locally with no key. Existing vaults backfill automatically in the background; new saves include it from the start. Also: chunker robustness fixes so long descriptions and coarse transcripts never lose their tail from search.

item.title: Youty 1.2.0
item.description:
Youty 1.2.0

MCP agentic loop: new view_frames tool lets your AI assistant see saved video frames directly — in any client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code), not just ones with filesystem access. Completes the search → read transcript → watch frames loop.

item.title: Youty 1.1.0
item.description:
Youty 1.1.0

On-device Core ML embeddings in the MCP server (drops PyTorch — smaller, faster install); Homebrew install now builds everywhere; hardened dependencies. Mac app functionally unchanged from 1.0.0.

item.title: Youty 1.0.0
item.description:
Youty 1.0.0 — initial public release

Turn YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok videos into a local, AI-readable knowledge base — 100% on-device.

  • Paste a URL for an instant transcript, or save the full video as a Markdown + frame-snapshot bundle in a vault you own.
  • Search everything by meaning, on-device (EmbeddingGemma + SigLIP). No API key, no account, no cloud, no telemetry.
  • Use it from the Mac app, the youty CLI, or any MCP-compatible AI assistant.

Requires macOS 26+ and Apple Silicon.
